description:
You are working as a penetration tester who has just finished an automated network scan.  
    The scan tool stored every finding inside a small SQLite database that already exists on the machine:

        /home/user/vulnscan/scan_results.db

    Inside that database there is a table named “vulnerabilities” created with the following schema:

        CREATE TABLE vulnerabilities(
            host_ip   TEXT,
            port      INTEGER,
            service   TEXT,
            cve_id    TEXT,
            severity  TEXT
        );

    Your next step is to extract the HIGH-severity findings so they can be passed to the remediation team.

    Produce a text file called  

        /home/user/vulnscan/high_severity.log  

    that meets ALL the following requirements:

    1. Retrieve every row whose column “severity” has the exact value “HIGH”.
    2. For each such row output only the columns in this exact order:
           host_ip | port | service | cve_id
       (do NOT include the “severity” column in the output).
    3. Use an ASCII pipe character “|” as the delimiter exactly as shown above.
    4. Do NOT output any header row.
    5. Sort the lines in ascending (lexicographic) order first by host_ip and then by port.
    6. End every line with a single UNIX newline, and make sure the file itself ends with a final newline.
    7. The file must reside at the precise path indicated and be world-readable (chmod 644 is sufficient).

    You can accomplish everything with a single invocation of the SQLite CLI (sqlite3) and standard shell redirection.

    When you are finished, /home/user/vulnscan/high_severity.log should exist and exactly match the format described above.  
    The automated test harness will:

       • open the file,  
       • verify its permissions,  
       • confirm that it contains the correctly sorted, pipe-delimited list of HIGH-severity findings with no header line.

    Only the contents of that log file are evaluated—any terminal output you print while working will be ignored by the grader.
truth:
<!--
    Pre-existing items on the filesystem before the agent starts.
    -->
    Expected existing directory:
        /home/user/vulnscan               (permissions 755)

    Expected pre-loaded SQLite database:
        /home/user/vulnscan/scan_results.db

    Table “vulnerabilities” inside the database contains exactly these rows:

        1. ("192.168.1.10", 22,   "ssh",   "CVE-2018-15473", "HIGH")
        2. ("192.168.1.12", 80,   "http",  "CVE-2020-5902",  "HIGH")
        3. ("192.168.1.20", 445,  "smb",   "CVE-2017-0144", "HIGH")
        4. ("192.168.1.30", 21,   "ftp",   "CVE-2021-41617", "MEDIUM")
        5. ("10.0.0.5",    3306, "mysql", "CVE-2016-6662",  "HIGH")

    The correct final artifact the agent must create:

        Path: /home/user/vulnscan/high_severity.log
        Permissions: 0644 (-rw-r--r--)
        Expected exact byte-for-byte contents:

            10.0.0.5|3306|mysql|CVE-2016-6662\n
            192.168.1.10|22|ssh|CVE-2018-15473\n
            192.168.1.12|80|http|CVE-2020-5902\n
            192.168.1.20|445|smb|CVE-2017-0144\n

        (The \n symbol above represents a single newline character; the file ends with the final newline.)

    The automated checker will:
        • open /home/user/vulnscan/high_severity.log,
        • confirm it exists and has mode 0644,
        • read its entire contents and compare to the exact four-line string shown above.
name: task_000000_29de7932
